global.title: Four sync architectures (A-D) trade server dependence against storage duplication
global.content:
Four architecture options lay out the full trade-space:

**A — Connect + Switchboard (current default)**
- PGlite <-> Switchboard (PostgreSQL) via GraphQL SyncManager
- Duplication: 2x
- Recovery: from Switchboard
- Requires: running a server

**B — Connect + Swarm plugin (no Switchboard)**
- PGlite -> Swarm via plugin
- Duplication: 2x
- Recovery: from Swarm (wallet signature)
- Requires: running a Bee node (local or remote)

**C — Connect + Switchboard + Swarm (both)**
- Both sync paths active
- Duplication: 3x
- Recovery: from either Switchboard OR Swarm
- Requires: server + Bee node

**D — Connect on Swarm (fully decentralized, end goal)**
- Connect SPA deployed to Swarm via ENS
- PGlite <-> Swarm via plugin (no Switchboard)
- Collaboration: SwarmChannel (peer-to-peer via feeds)
- Duplication: 2x (same as B and A)
- Recovery: from Swarm (wallet signature)
- Requires: wallet + local Bee node
- Servers: ZERO

**Key insight**: the end-goal architecture (D) has **identical 2x duplication** to the current default (A). Going fully decentralized does not cost extra storage — it just swaps where the remote copy lives (server PostgreSQL -> Swarm network).
global.description: Architectures A (Switchboard), B (Swarm plugin), C (both), D (fully decentralized Connect-on-Swarm) differ in duplication cost and server requirements.
